Fireflies.ai vs Tally: Which Is Better?

Choosing between Fireflies.ai and Tally? Here's a side-by-side comparison to help you decide.

Quick verdict: Choose Fireflies.ai if you need remote-teams or sales-teams. Choose Tally if you prioritize freelancers or small-teams.

Fireflies.ai

PricingFree / $10/mo Pro / $19/mo Business
Rating★★★★ 4.2/5

Key Features

  • Automatic meeting recording and transcription across Zoom, Meet, Teams
  • AI-powered meeting summaries with key decisions and action items
  • Searchable meeting history across your entire team
  • Soundbites for clipping and sharing specific moments from recordings
  • Conversation intelligence for sales teams and deal analysis
  • Integrations with Notion, Slack, Asana, Salesforce, and HubSpot

Pros

  • Auto-joins meetings and records them without anyone needing to remember or click anything
  • Transcription accuracy is good for clean audio, though it struggles with heavy accents or overlapping speech
  • AI summaries capture key decisions, action items, and questions in a digestible format
  • Search works across your entire meeting history — type 'budget decision' and find the exact conversation
  • Integrates with Slack, Notion, Salesforce, and Asana for pushing notes into your workflow automatically

Cons

  • Free tier is limited to 3 meetings total — not enough for a proper evaluation
  • Transcription accuracy varies significantly with background noise, speaker overlap, or accents
  • Privacy concerns about having an AI bot in every meeting can unsettle team members or clients
  • Action item detection is useful but not reliable enough to trust completely; you'll still need to verify
  • The web app can be slow with large meeting histories; searching across 500+ recordings lags
Try Fireflies.ai →

Tally

PricingFree / $29/mo Pro
Rating★★★★ 4.4/5

Key Features

  • Document-style form builder with clean, professional design
  • Conditional logic and branching for dynamic forms
  • Unlimited forms and responses on free tier
  • Embedding, popup, and link sharing options
  • 800+ integrations via Zapier, Make, and native connections
  • Collaboration features for team form building

Pros

  • Forms look like real documents, not templates; the design is clean and professional by default
  • Free tier is seriously generous — unlimited forms, unlimited responses, no watermark
  • Logic jumps and conditional fields work well for complex forms and surveys
  • Integrations with Notion, Slack, Google Sheets, and 500+ tools via Zapier/Make
  • You can embed forms anywhere or use Tally's hosted pages (which look great on mobile)

Cons

  • Design customization is limited compared to Typeform or Jotform; you get Tally's look and that's it
  • No native payment collection built in; you'll need to embed Stripe or PayPal separately
  • File uploads on free tier are limited to 10MB; Pro bumps it to 100MB
  • No native analytics beyond basic response tracking
  • The Pro plan ($29/mo) is pricey for what is essentially a form builder

Which Should You Choose?

Both Fireflies.ai and Tally are excellent tools in their category. The right choice depends on your specific needs:

  • Choose Fireflies.ai if: Fireflies is the AI meeting assistant that joins your calls, records everything, transcribes the conversation, and generates summaries. It works with Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, and Webex — and it's gotten good enough that you'll stop taking notes entirely. The searchable history is the killer feature: you can find any decision, commitment, or name mentioned across hundreds of past meetings.
  • Choose Tally if: Tally is the form builder that looks like a document. Instead of the clunky drag-and-drop builders that produce obviously templated forms, Tally lets you write your form like you're writing a page — with real text, headings, and dividers between form fields. It's a small idea that makes a huge difference in how professional your forms look. And the free tier is genuinely generous.
We may earn a commission if you purchase through links. All opinions are our own.
